title: Transmission electron microscopy of fibroblasts
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1713o","text":"This protocol describes the procedure for preparing and imaging cultured cells by transmission electron microscopy used in the Seifert Lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fix cells by replacing the growth medium with a fixative solution containing 4% Paraformaldehyde solution Contributed by users and 3.5% EM-grade Glutaraldehyde Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #16220 in 0.1 M Sorensens Phosphate Buffer Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #11600 at 4 °C
Replace solution with fresh fixative solution
Wash cells thoroughly with 0.1 M Sorensens Phosphate Buffer Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #11600
Post-fix in 1% Osmium Tetroxide Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #19134 (CAS: 20816-12-0) for 01:00:00 at 4 °C
Dehydrate sample in a graded ethanol series from 50% through 100% for 00:05:00 each
Wash twice with 100% Ethanol Contributed by users at 4 °C
Infiltrate with epoxy resin directly in the culture dish
Transfer into beam capsules and embed overnight
Section polymerized blocks at 80-90 nm using an ultramicrotome
Mount sections on 300-mesh copper EM grids
Stain with Uranyl acetate Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #22400 and Lead Citrate (Reynolds) Delta Microscopies Catalog #11300 to enhance contrast
Image on a transmission electron microscope
creator: Ashley Seifert
